value.found_quote: | `mcp` | `mcp(server/tool)`, `mcp(server/*)`, or `mcp(*)` | Matches exact MCP tools or all tools on a specified server (applies equally to local `mcpl` servers and remote connections). Using `mcp(*)` matches any tool. | **Ask** |
value.rationale: The page explicitly lists `mcp` as a supported permission action in the "Supported Actions & Matching Rules" table. It describes it as governing access to MCP tools on both local (`mcpl`) servers and remote connections, matching the claim's description of "a permission action governing agent access to MCP tools across local and remote MCP servers." Configuration examples further reinforce this, showing `mcp(linter/*)` in the Allow list and `mcp(sql/execute_mutation)` in the Ask list. The page presents this as a currently active feature (no preview or beta label is attached to the `mcp` permission itself).
